designs.area: SEARCH DEPTH â€” wire evidence_deepener into the launch sweep (#942) + raise/retune fetch cap safely (#943). Goal: close the evidence-depth gap to Gemini 3.1 Pro DR / ChatGPT 5.5 Pro DR by adding citation-snowball + recommendation + mechanism depth to Pipeline A, WITHOUT weakening strict_verify or the D8 release gate, and WITHOUT introducing any unconditional runtime spend.

best_practices.practice: Agentic deep research is an iterative retrieveâ†’reasonâ†’reformulate loop, NOT single-pass: evidence found in step N seeds the queries of step N+1 (citation-chasing, named-study/author search, mechanism queries). This is exactly what evidence_deepener.py implements (backward+forward S2 snowball + recommendations + mechanism search).
best_practices.source: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.18959v1 (From Web Search towards Agentic Deep ReSearch, 2026) + https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202511.2077 (Deep Research: A Systematic Survey, 2026)
best_practices.why: Confirms the deepener's design is the correct shape for closing the depth gap; the fix is wiring an existing best-practice engine into the launch path, not inventing one.

best_practices.practice: Adaptive / value-based STOPPING is the key missing component in agentic RAG: don't always-deepen. Trigger further retrieval only when a computed signal says marginal benefit is high (e.g. corpus still inadequate or completeness gap remains); stop when adequate. Always-on iterative retrieval wastes budget and dilutes context on already-sufficient corpora.
best_practices.source: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.14337 (Stop-RAG: Value-Based Retrieval Control for Iterative RAG, 2025) + https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202511.2077
best_practices.why: Justifies gating the deepener behind a conditional trigger (post-R-6 completeness/adequacy borderline) rather than running it on every query, and defaulting the sweep wiring OFF until proven â€” protects the 720s/budget envelope.

best_practices.practice: Test-time scaling has a breadth/depth tradeoff: tokens spent on retrieval reduce tokens available for reasoning, so more sources is not strictly better â€” raise source count only with dedup + relevance filtering + a hard cap, and let a budget constraint bind. Precision (provenance-verifiable spans) beats raw source count in high-stakes settings.
best_practices.source: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.18959v1 (test-time scaling trade-offs section) + clinical-safety Â§-1.1 (counts are banned as a quality signal)
best_practices.why: Directly informs #943: raise the cap but bind it to PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN (Invariant 9.1.6) and keep dedup + _filter_by_query_relevance; do not treat 'more sources' as the win condition â€” strict_verify-passing spans are the win condition.

best_practices.practice: ReAct-style reformulation: agents alternate reasoning and tool-use, deriving the next query from the content just read (named studies, mechanisms) instead of fixed query templates. Deduplicate aggressively across hops; cap per-seed fan-out to avoid combinatorial blowup.
best_practices.why: The deepener already does this (OP-1 named-study extraction â†’ OP-3 author/citation search; per-seed caps PG_DEEPENER_REFS_PER_SEED=10, dedup by paperId/url in _finalize). Reuse as-is; no new reformulation logic needed.

current_state.what: Pipeline A launch path. Single primary retrieval pass via run_live_retrieval(...) with PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER=8, PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2=8, PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP=20. Already has ONE-SHOT gap-triggered expansion (R-6, lines 1830-1901): when completeness leaves uncovered topics it fires a second run_live_retrieval, then MERGES results by deduping URLs against existing, renumbering evidence_ids as ev_{base+i:03d}, appending to classified_sources + evidence_rows, recomputing compute_tier_distribution + assess_corpus_adequacy. The deepener is NOT wired here.

The real depth engine (cap 150). deepen_evidence(client, state) reads ONLY 3 state keys (confirmed by grep): iteration_count, evidence (list of dicts with statement/direct_quote/source_url), original_query. Returns {'deepened_papers': [...S2 paper dicts...], 'deepener_stats': {...}}. Already has full timeout/budget/dedup/relevance-filter machinery (PG_DEEPENER_TIMEOUT=720, per-op timeouts, _finalize dedup, _filter_by_query_relevance with embedding+keyword fallback). No-ops cleanly if SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY is absent (line 111-114) â€” this is the proof of zero unconditional spend. Wired ONLY into graph.py/v2/v3.

Pipeline B's existing consumer of the deepener. Shows the REUSE pattern for the output->evidence conversion: deepened_papers are merged into academic_results (dedup by url) and their full_text is injected into the SQLite content cache so the analyzer re-fetches nothing. This is a reference for the conversion shim but is graph/state-specific, not directly callable from the sweep.

CONFIRMED: fetch_cap is a TOTAL cap, not per-query. Line 1307 total_pre_filter=len(candidates) over the deduped union of all queries; line 1324 candidates=candidates[:fetch_cap + _n_seed_injected]. The sweep comment at line 1587 ('max URLs ... per query') is WRONG; the docstring line 1185 ('Hard cap on total URLs to fetch') is correct â€” a doc bug to fix in #943. Library default DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP=40 but the sweep overrides DOWN to 20. evidence_rows are built (lines 1556-1564) with evidence_id/source_url/statement/direct_quote/tier via classify_source_tier + is_content_starved + _build_provenance_quote(head_chars=1500, window_chars=500) â€” the ONLY strict_verify-compatible entry path.

process_retrieval is NETWORK-FREE BY DESIGN: default fetch_fn is a sentinel that raises NotImplementedError; the real Serper+S2 fetch_fn is deferred to PR 7. evidence_pool.Source carries full_text: str|None = None, full_text_available=False, provenance={} â€” CONFIRMED no provenance spans at retrieval time. Therefore clinical-path deepened evidence cannot pass strict_verify until PR 7 ships a fetch_fn with provenance. The clinical-path half is necessarily thinner than the sweep half.

designs.proposed_solution:
Split into two PRs matching the issue split and the 200-LOC halt cap. CORE IDEA: the deepener wiring is structurally 'R-6 gap-expansion, but the new evidence comes from citation-snowball instead of completeness gaps' â€” reuse the existing R-6 merge code (lines 1855-1899) rather than inventing a new merge.

PR #942 (the bulk â€” wire the deepener into the sweep behind a flag, conditional trigger):
1. Add a new module src/polaris_graph/retrieval/deepener_sweep_adapter.py with two pure, offline-testable functions: (a) build_deepener_state(evidence_rows, question) -> the 3-key state dict {iteration_count:0, evidence: evidence_rows, original_query: question}; (b) deepened_papers_to_evidence_rows(deepened_papers, existing_urls, base_index, classify_fn, build_quote_fn) -> list[evidence_row] that routes EVERY deepened paper through the SAME strict_verify-compatible path used by live_retriever: classify_source_tier (drop if it doesn't classify â€” S2 returns off-allowlist domains), is_content_starved (drop), _build_provenance_quote(head_chars=1500, window_chars=500) over paper['full_text'] (papers with only an abstract and no fetched full_text produce thin/empty quotes and are dropped â€” correct fail-closed behavior, NOT a bug). Renumber ev_{base+i:03d}. This shim is the clinical-safety chokepoint.
2. In run_honest_sweep_r3.py, AFTER the R-6 expansion block and BEFORE the adequacy==abort gate, add a flag-gated call: if PG_SWEEP_EVIDENCE_DEEPENER=1 AND SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY present AND a conditional trigger fires (corpus borderline: adequacy.decision != 'abort' but completeness.total_uncovered>0 OR adequacy has any warn finding), run asyncio.run(deepen_evidence(client, build_deepener_state(...))) using the SAME cost-tracked OpenRouterClient the sweep already constructs (so Invariant 9.1.6 budget cap binds on its LLM calls), convert via the shim, merge via the EXISTING R-6 dedup/append/renumber/re-compute-tier/re-assess code path (refactored into a small _merge_new_evidence helper shared by R-6 and the deepener). Default flag OFF for launch until the shim is proven offline.
3. Wrap the whole deepener call in try/except that logs and continues (fail-open on the OPTIONAL enrichment is correct â€” absence must be byte-identical to today; the deepener NEVER weakens a gate, it only adds candidates that must independently pass strict_verify + D8).

PR #943 (small â€” cap retune + doc fix):
1. Fix the wrong sweep comment at line 1587 to say 'total URLs to fetch & classify (across all queries, after dedup)'.
2. Raise PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP default from 20 to 40 (matching the library DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP=40) and bump PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER/PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2 from 8 to e.g. 12, keeping them env-overridable (LAW VI). The budget cap PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN (Invariant 9.1.6, _impute_cost_from_tokens backstop) remains the hard ceiling, so raising the fetch cap cannot blow the cost envelope â€” it just lets the corpus grow until the budget binds. Document the breadth/depth tradeoff in the comment and keep dedup + _filter_by_query_relevance on.

CLINICAL PATH (honest scope, defaults OFF, depends on PR 7): add an OPTIONAL post-assess() enrichment in clinical_retriever behind the SAME flag + S2-key â€” route deepened papers through classify_url allowlist + _dedupe_by_url + re-assess(). Because Source carries no provenance spans until PR 7's fetch_fn ships, deepened clinical evidence cannot yet pass strict_verify; the design states this explicitly rather than implying parity with the sweep. The deepener calling S2 directly via aiohttp also conflicts with clinical_retriever's network-free-by-design contract, so the clinical enrichment is gated, defaults off, and is documented as PR-7-dependent â€” it does NOT block the sweep half.

risks: Provenance impedance: deepened_papers are S2 dicts, not strict_verify-ready evidence_rows. If the conversion shim is wrong, fabricated/thin-quote papers could slip past strict_verify. MITIGATION: route every paper through the identical classify_source_tier + is_content_starved + _build_provenance_quote path; drop on any failure (fail-closed). Unit-test the drops.

Clinical-path network-free contract: the deepener calls S2 directly via aiohttp, violating clinical_retriever's sentinel-fetch_fn isolation, and Source has no provenance until PR 7. MITIGATION: clinical enrichment is flag-gated, defaults OFF, documented PR-7-dependent; the sweep half ships independently.

Budget/time blowout: deepener has a 720s budget and cap 150; running it on every query could dilute context and burn the cost envelope. MITIGATION: conditional trigger (only when corpus borderline) per Stop-RAG; PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN binds the LLM cost via the same cost-tracked client; default flag OFF for launch.

fetch_cap semantics were mis-documented (total vs per-query). Raising it interacts with seed-injection (_n_seed_injected additive). MITIGATION: #943 fixes the comment and keeps the +seed additive behavior; the budget cap is the real ceiling.

_filter_by_query_relevance tries to import embedding_service; the sweep deliberately keeps enable_prefetch_filter=False for speed/RAM (Â§8.4). MITIGATION: confirmed the deepener's filter degrades to keyword-overlap fallback on ImportError, so no heavy model load is forced; document this; do NOT enable embeddings unconditionally.
evidence_id collision on merge: deepened rows must be renumbered base+i like R-6 does. MITIGATION: shared _merge_new_evidence helper renumbers consistently for both paths.

D8 release gate / strict_verify must never be weakened: the deepener only ADDS candidates. MITIGATION: state explicitly that deepened evidence is subject to identical strict_verify + D8; no gate threshold changes in either PR.

designs.area: Clinical retrieval backend (depth, #942 sub-part) â€” add a clinical domain branch to src/polaris_graph/retrieval/domain_backends.py that plugs Europe PMC into the SHIPPING path (run_honest_sweep_r3 -> live_retriever.run_live_retrieval -> run_domain_backends) alongside Serper+S2. Defer ClinicalTrials.gov (runtime 403) and openFDA labels (needs tier_classifier allowlist change) to Codex-reviewed fast-follows.

best_practices.practice: Europe PMC RESTful search (keyless) is the highest-leverage single clinical backend: one keyless GET returns per-result pmid + pmcid + doi + fullTextUrlList across 30M+ pubs indexing PubMed, NICE, Agricola. Endpoint https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search; params query, format=json, resultType=core, pageSize, sort (sort_cited:y / sort_date:y). No API key required.
best_practices.why: Keyless, full-text-resolving, and subsumes much of PubMed in a single call â€” minimizes LOC and network calls while delivering primary RCT/SR literature for the clinical questions. Empirically verified 200 + pmcid/doi fields present for a tirzepatide query on 2026-05-29.

best_practices.practice: PubMed E-utilities two-step (esearch.fcgi -> PMIDs -> efetch.fcgi rettype=abstract/medline). Base https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/{esearch,efetch}.fcgi; params db=pubmed, term, retmax, retmode=json, api_key (optional). Rate limit 3 req/s keyless, 10 req/s with api_key. Use Entrez History for batch efetch.
best_practices.why: NCBI_API_KEY is present in .env but unused; E-utilities is the canonical PubMed primary-literature API. Codebase already uses efetch.fcgi in frame_fetcher.py:196 (precedent). Kept as an optional NCBI_API_KEY-gated supplement (default-off flag) to stay under the 200-LOC cap this PR.

best_practices.practice: openFDA drug label API (api.fda.gov/drug/label.json) is keyless, 240 req/min + 1000/day per IP keyless (240/min + 120000/day with key). Returns full Structured Product Labeling text inline (indications_and_usage, dosage_and_administration, contraindications, warnings_and_cautions) plus openfda.application_number and set_id.
best_practices.why: Drug labels are uniquely authoritative for dosing/contraindication/safety claims that the clinical-safety questions need (T1). DEFERRED this PR: the inline JSON text is invisible to strict_verify (pipeline only fetches cand.url), and the only fetchable full-label home is DailyMed (706KB, all sections), which is NOT in the tier_classifier allowlist â€” adding it is a separate review surface.

best_practices.practice: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 (https://clinicaltrials.gov/api/v2/studies) with query.cond/query.intr/query.term, filter.phase=PHASE1..4, filter.overallStatus, pageSize (max 1000, default 10), pageToken pagination, format=json. Replaced the classic API (retired June 2024).
best_practices.why: Authoritative trial registry (T3) for RCT design/status. DEFERRED: empirically returns HTTP 403 (Akamai bot block) from this runtime with both POLARIS-UA and browser-UA on both the SPA study page and the v2 API on 2026-05-29 â€” committing it would add failed-fetch noise, not evidence. Capture as a fast-follow with UA/headers + fail-open hardening.

best_practices.practice: Rate-limit with a fixed, deterministic backoff schedule (e.g. frame_fetcher.py _BACKOFF_SECONDS = (1.0, 2.0, 4.0)) inside the backend, not bare time.sleep() to simulate work.
best_practices.why: CLAUDE.md Â§9.4 forbids time.sleep() to simulate work and the test suite detects it; a deterministic inter-request delay honoring the 3 req/s keyless / 10 req/s keyed PubMed limit reuses the existing in-repo pattern and stays test-clean.

designs.proposed_solution: Add a fail-open, keyless Europe PMC clinical backend to domain_backends.py and wire it into run_domain_backends under a new `elif domain == "clinical":` branch â€” the single verified, highest-authority, full-text-resolving primary-literature source for the clinical benchmark questions. Empirically grounded decisions: (1) Europe PMC is keyless and returns pmcid/doi/fullTextUrlList per hit; resolve each hit to an ALLOWLISTED fetchable URL â€” prefer pmcid -> https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC<id>/ (verified 180KB, tiers T2), else doi -> https://doi.org/<doi> (best-effort; doi-only hits may resolve to a paywalled landing page and T7-stub â€” stated honestly, not implied as full text), and SKIP hits with neither (same discipline as the S2 path). Never emit a europepmc.org landing URL (not allowlisted -> UNKNOWN drop). (2) Defer ClinicalTrials.gov (runtime 403/Akamai on this network, verified) and openFDA labels (label text is inline JSON which the pipeline cannot consume â€” it only fetches cand.url â€” and the full-label fetchable home DailyMed is not allowlisted) to two Codex-reviewed fast-follow issues, so this PR stays under the 200-LOC halt cap and ships only what is end-to-end verified. (3) Keep an optional PubMed E-utilities (esearch+efetch) supplement behind a default-OFF env flag (PG_CLINICAL_ENABLE_PUBMED=0) that uses NCBI_API_KEY when present for the 10 req/s tier â€” wired but dormant so it adds no runtime behavior or LOC pressure until a follow-up enables it. The backend is fail-open (any exception -> empty list + log, never breaks retrieval), rate-limited with the deterministic frame_fetcher backoff pattern (no time.sleep-to-simulate-work), snake_case, explicit imports, no except:pass, no unittest.mock in src. It changes ZERO behavior until a clinical sweep runs (flag/branch only), makes only FREE keyless calls at benchmark time, never weakens strict_verify or the D8 release gate (it only ADDS authoritative candidates that pass the SAME fetch/tier/adequacy gates).

designs.area: Qualitative / directional clinical conflict detection (#944) â€” add a deterministic assertion-disagreement path beside the numeric-regex detector in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/contradiction_detector.py, surfaced into contradictions.json + the report disclosure block, with an OPT-IN local-NLI annotation layer (no spend, no network).

best_practices.practice: Assertion-status detection (Present / Absent / Hypothetical / Conditional / Associated-with-someone-else), not bare negation, is the correct upstream primitive for clinical 'present-vs-absent' conflicts. NegEx-style rule cues remain the high-precision backbone; ML/LLM only add recall.
best_practices.source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.17425 (Beyond Negation Detection: Comprehensive Assertion Detection Models for Clinical NLP, 2025) â€” fine-tuned model 0.962 acc vs GPT-4o 0.901; biggest gains on Absent (+8.4%) and Hypothetical (+23.4%).
best_practices.why: The patient-dangerous conflicts are exactly contraindication/interaction/eligibility PRESENT vs ABSENT. Detecting the assertion polarity (and excluding hypothetical/conditional/other-subject) is what separates a real conflict from noise. Validates a rule-cue layer as the precision backbone rather than a pure semantic model.

best_practices.practice: Do NOT rely on NLI/dense semantics to catch negation â€” negation collapses in embedding space ('Semantic Collapse'). Pair explicit rule-based negation/assertion cues WITH (optionally) calibrated NLI; never NLI-alone for negation.
best_practices.source: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17580 (Negation is Not Semantic: Diagnosing Dense Retrieval Failure Modes for Contradiction-Aware Biomedical QA, 2026).
best_practices.why: Directly justifies making the deterministic rule path the primary detector and relegating NLI to an annotation/confidence layer, not the flag gate. A pure-NLI design would silently miss the negated contraindications this fix exists to catch.

best_practices.practice: Use a cascade: high-precision rule filter â†’ calibrated NLI â†’ (optional) judge, with abstain. NLI-only = high recall/low precision; hybrid reaches ~92% acc / 89.5 F1. Surfacing dissent with high precision is the explicit TREC 2025 BioGen 'contradiction-aware grounding' task.
best_practices.source: https://www.shadecoder.com/topics/contradiction-detection-a-comprehensive-guide-for-2025 and TREC 2025 BioGen contradiction-aware grounding (referenced in https://aclanthology.org/2023.emnlp-main.80.pdf Clinical Contradiction Detection).
best_practices.why: The numeric detector scored 0/3 precision precisely because it had no precision filter (over-grouping). The cascade pattern â€” restrictive deterministic gate first â€” is the competitive lever vs Gemini/ChatGPT DR, which do not surface high-precision dissent. Precision, not recall, wins the clinical benchmark.

best_practices.practice: Require explicit assertion on BOTH sides and â‰¥2 distinct sources before flagging a conflict; silence is not an 'absent' assertion; dedupe identical spans/URLs.
best_practices.source: Clinical Contradiction Detection, EMNLP 2023 https://aclanthology.org/2023.emnlp-main.80.pdf (decoupled high-precision support vs high-recall contradiction with negation-aware filtering); reinforced by advisor review of real contradictions.json.
best_practices.why: The real contradictions.json shows ev_002 and ev_021 are the IDENTICAL quote from the IDENTICAL URL flagged as a contradiction. 'Explicit-present vs explicit-absent only, â‰¥2 distinct source_urls, span dedup' is the single biggest flood-preventer and is what keeps contradictions.json from drowning the report.

current_state.what: detect_contradictions() is NUMERIC-ONLY: it groups ExtractedNumericClaim by (subject, predicate, unit, dose) and flags when relative+absolute numeric thresholds trip. No qualitative/directional/assertion path exists. The module docstring (lines 15-19) explicitly says it is deterministic rule-based and deliberately avoids LLMs for NUMERIC contradictions.

ExtractedNumericClaim and ContradictionRecord dataclasses. ContradictionRecord exposes .subject, .predicate, .claims, .severity, .recommended_action â€” these are the fields the report block and PT08 consume. A sibling qualitative record must expose subject/predicate/claims to flow through unchanged.

Integration point: extract_numeric_claims(...) then detect_contradictions(...), writes [asdict(c) for c in contradictions] to contradictions.json, logs counts. This is where the qualitative path must be invoked and its records concatenated into the same `contradictions` list.

Report disclosure block (M-22/M-25e). Iterates `for c in contradictions` using getattr-with-dict-fallback on subject/predicate/claims â€” so a duck-type-compatible sibling record renders here with NO code change. PT08 requires subject+predicate verbatim in report; the loop already emits them.

PT08 rule check reads c.get('subject')/c.get('predicate') from the asdict'd records and looks for them + a disclosure keyword in report text. Confirms the new record must serialize with 'subject' and 'predicate' keys; no other evaluator change needed.

REUSABLE local NLI (MiniCheck/flan-t5-large via transformers, torch already in requirements.txt:81-83). PG_NLI_ENABLED defaults to '0' (off). load_nli_model() lazy-loads, returns None and the caller falls back gracefully if unavailable. This is the no-network/no-spend annotation engine â€” but currently async and built for evidence-vs-source, not claim-vs-claim.

_EntailmentJudge is OpenRouter-based (network + spend) AND fails OPEN (returns ENTAILED on error). DISQUALIFIED as a runtime escalation target under the NO-SPEND/NO-NETWORK/fail-closed constraints. Noted explicitly so the constraint override is visible to Codex, not an oversight.

The real 0/3-precision output: all 3 flags are false positives â€” (1) ev_002==ev_021 identical quote/URL grouped with itself + an achievement-threshold '5%' mislabeled as a value; (2) HbA1c-2.4% vs weight-22.5% conflated under 'weight loss'; (3) HbA1c reductions mislabeled 'weight loss' and a '6.5% or less' threshold pulled as a value. Empirical proof the gaps are over-grouping + threshold-as-value, not a qualitative bug â€” used as negative controls in tests.

designs.proposed_solution:
Add a deterministic QUALITATIVE assertion-disagreement path to contradiction_detector.py, beside (not replacing) the numeric path, surfaced into the SAME contradictions.json + report block. Default-on deterministic; opt-in local-NLI annotation. Three layers:

LAYER 1 â€” Assertion extraction (deterministic, NO network/spend). New extract_qualitative_assertions(evidence, domain) returns QualitativeAssertion(evidence_id, subject, relation, polarity, source_url, source_tier, span, cue). `relation` comes from a TIGHT curated CLINICAL-SAFETY vocab only: contraindication, drug_interaction_warning, eligibility/exclusion (no cross-domain generalization â€” the benchmark is clinical). `polarity` âˆˆ {present, absent}; derived from explicit cues: a NegEx-style _ABSENT_CUE_RE ((is |are )?(not |never )?(contraindicat|interact|eligibl|exclud)... preceded/followed by no|not|never|without|absent|no evidence of|safe in|may be used in / vs explicit present cues (contraindicated in, must not, do not use, avoid in, interacts with). Exclude hypothetical/conditional/other-subject cue windows (if, may, possible, in patients who, history of) per arxiv 2503.17425 assertion classes â€” these are NOT present/absent assertions.

LAYER 2 â€” Conflict pairing (deterministic, fail-CLOSED toward SURFACING). New detect_qualitative_conflicts(assertions). Flag a QualitativeConflictRecord iff: (a) same normalized (subject, relation); (b) one side explicit polarity=present AND the other explicit polarity=absent (SILENCE is never 'absent'); (c) â‰¥2 DISTINCT source_url; (d) span/evidence_id dedup so the ev_002==ev_021 class can never fire. Record is duck-type-compatible: exposes .subject, .predicate (= relation label, e.g. 'contraindication (present vs absent)'), .claims (the two QualitativeAssertion sides adapted to expose evidence_id/source_tier/value-or-snippet), .severity ('high' for contraindication/interaction, 'medium' for eligibility), .recommended_action. Appended to the same `contradictions` list at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2078.

LAYER 3 â€” OPTIONAL local-NLI annotation (opt-in, default OFF via new PG_QUAL_CONFLICT_NLI=0, mirrors PG_NLI_ENABLED). When enabled AND the local MiniCheck model is present (HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 / local_files_only enforced so transformers cannot reach the network), run claim-vs-claim NLI on the two flagged spans to ANNOTATE record.nli_label/nli_confidence and refine severity. CRITICAL: NLI only annotates the already-flagged set â€” it NEVER suppresses a deterministic flag (suppression would be fail-OPEN for a disclosure detector; for a disclosure surface fail-closed = SURFACE). If NLI can't load, records still surface unchanged.

The LLM-judge half of the task brief is explicitly OUT: _EntailmentJudge is network+spend+fail-open, all three disqualifying. The runtime escalation target is local-only, opt-in, annotate-only. no_spend = true and provable: the default shipping path is pure-Python regex/string logic; the NLI layer is off by default and offline-pinned when on.

designs.area: Retrieval observability (#945) â€” per-call retrieval_trace.jsonl mirroring the I-gen-004 reasoning-sink pattern, so a reviewer can reconstruct the full search session (per query: backend + query text + return count + returned URLs; per kept source: originating backend; per drop: URL + reason).

best_practices.practice: Adopt OpenTelemetry GenAI retrieval span vocabulary as the JSONL field names: a per-query record carries gen_ai.operation.name="retrieval", a data-source/backend id, the query text (gen_ai.retrieval.query.text), and a documents list where each returned document is at minimum {id, score}. Map POLARIS fields to these (backend->data_source.id, query->query.text, returned URLs->documents[].id).
best_practices.why: Using the standard 2026 schema makes the artifact tool-agnostic and reviewer-legible; the spec defines retrieved-document records as {id (string), score (double)} which is exactly the per-query return list a reviewer needs to replay a search.

best_practices.practice: For RAG retrieval observability, record per-retrieval (retriever.strategy = serper|s2|domain, top_k, index/version) and per-document (source_url, similarity/relevance score, snippet, doc_version). A reviewer replays a session from the list of (id, score, snippet) + the strategy + top_k.
best_practices.why: Confirms the minimum reconstruction set the task asks for (backend, query, count, URLs per query; originating backend per kept source) is exactly current best practice; aligns field names so the artifact reads like a standard RAG trace.

best_practices.practice: Treat a wrong answer as often a RETRIEVAL failure, not a generation failure â€” so retrieval traces must be as rich as generation traces, and must explicitly capture DROPPED/filtered candidates with the rejection reason (the one gap the OTel spec and most tools leave to custom events).
best_practices.why: Justifies the per-drop record (URL + reason: duplicate_url, off_topic_prefilter, content_starved, fetch_failed, over_fetch_cap, scope_query_dropped). Drop transparency is the highest-value reviewer signal and is precisely what record_retrieval_attempt (name-set only) cannot provide today.

best_practices.practice: Best-effort, run-scoped, duck-typed sink that NEVER raises; write-through to disk on every record so the artifact is current no matter which abort/error path the run exits; fail loud only on schema/wiring mistakes (unknown enum), never on a capture error.
best_practices.why: This is POLARIS's own proven, Codex-APPROVE'd observability pattern (reasoning_trace.jsonl). Mirroring it gives a Codex-reviewable design with zero new architectural risk and guarantees retrieval capture can never break the shipping Pipeline A run.

current_state.what: record_retrieval_attempt(backend) adds only a backend NAME string to a set (_RETRIEVAL contextvar); attempted_backends() returns that set. No query text, no return count, no URLs, no per-kept-source backend, no drop reasons. The Path-B gate (pathB_runner.py:201, assert_post_run) consumes ONLY the name set â€” so the observability gap is orthogonal to the gate and can be added without touching it.

_serper_search and _s2_bulk_search are the per-query backend calls; each already calls _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt(name) lazily/best-effort (lines 95, 130). run_live_retrieval (1164-1576) loops effective_queries calling both, builds candidates with .source (serper|s2|primary_trial_doi|domain), dedups via seen_urls (drop=duplicate), applies prefetch off-topic filter (1311-1318, drop=off_topic), slices to fetch_cap (1324, drop=over_cap), and per candidate fetches (ok/failed, 1462-1466) and skips content-starved (1547-1551, drop=content_starved). All per-query/per-URL/per-drop data already exists at these points; it is simply discarded.

Domain (policy/tech/DD) backends also call record_retrieval_attempt("serper"); run_domain_backends returns per_backend_counts + candidates that run_live_retrieval merges (1286-1300). Same emit points apply; the trace must cover domain backends to be complete.

The exact wiring template: a ReasoningTraceCollector(out_dir=run_dir) is built, flush(run_dir) materializes the (possibly empty) jsonl, set_reasoning_sink(collector) registers it; released via set_reasoning_sink(None) at every exit (1578/1735/1974/2066/2705/3488). run_live_retrieval is called at 1656 with run_dir in scope. A RetrievalTraceCollector wires identically right beside it.

ReasoningTraceCollector is the dumb thread-safe append/update/write-through store to copy. Tests show the offline no-live test style (register/clear fixture, assert sink contents). bundle_schema.ContentType + conformance + manifest_builder show that bundling an artifact needs a content_type literal + JSONL conformance entry â€” relevant if/when retrieval_trace is added to the signed audit bundle (can be a deferred follow-up; the jsonl-in-run_dir artifact ships first).

designs.proposed_solution: Add a run-scoped, write-through, best-effort RetrievalTraceCollector that mirrors the I-gen-004 ReasoningTraceCollector, and a duck-typed sink seam in pathB_capture.py, producing retrieval_trace.jsonl in run_dir. Three JSONL record kinds (frozen 'kind' enum so the schema is uniform): (1) kind=\"query\" â€” one per backend invocation: {backend, query_text, return_count, returned_urls:[...], top_k, ts}; (2) kind=\"kept\" â€” one per source that survives into classified_sources/evidence_rows: {url, origin_backend, source, tier?, ts}; (3) kind=\"drop\" â€” one per discarded candidate: {url, origin_backend, reason, ts} where reason is a frozen vocabulary {duplicate_url, scope_query_dropped, off_topic_prefilter, over_fetch_cap, fetch_failed, content_starved}. Field NAMES alias the OTel GenAI retrieval conventions (operation.name=retrieval implied by file; backend=gen_ai.data_source.id; query_text=gen_ai.retrieval.query.text; returned_urls map to gen_ai.retrieval.documents[].id) so the artifact reads as a standard 2026 RAG trace, while the drop records are POLARIS's documented extension over the spec (which omits rejection transparency). The collector is a dumb thread-safe append+write-through store (no business logic). pathB_capture.py gains record_retrieval_query/record_kept_source/record_dropped_source thin wrappers that no-op unless a sink is registered (exactly like the existing record_retrieval_attempt no-ops unless _RETRIEVAL is set), keeping live_retriever's import surface unchanged (it already imports pathB_capture lazily). live_retriever.run_live_retrieval and domain_backends emit at the points where the data already exists (query lists at 1241/1257/domain; dedup at 1247/1262/1289; scope drop in validate_amplified_queries result; off-topic at 1313; cap at 1324; fetch fail at 1463; content-starved at 1547). The sink is registered/released in run_honest_sweep_r3.py right beside set_reasoning_sink, write-through to run_dir/retrieval_trace.jsonl, flushed empty up-front so an abort still yields the artifact. NOTHING touches the D8 release gate, strict_verify, the Path-B identity gate, or record_retrieval_attempt (kept as-is for the gate). NO network, NO spend â€” pure local capture of data already in memory. Capture NEVER raises; unknown kind/reason fails loud only inside the collector unit tests (wiring-bug guard), best-effort at the call sites.

risks: contextvar visibility across asyncio child tasks: run_live_retrieval is synchronous but parallel_fetch uses a ThreadPool â€” contextvars are copied per-thread at submit time, so a sink set in the parent IS visible to worker threads (read-only), but appends from worker threads must go through the collector's own lock (the collector is the shared mutable, not the contextvar). Mitigation: the collector holds the lock (mirrors ReasoningTraceCollector which is already thread-safe); emit kept/drop from the synchronous post-fetch loop (parent thread) not inside the fetch worker, so per-URL records are single-threaded anyway.

Double-counting / record explosion: fetch_cap=40, max_serper/s2=20 per query, multiple queries => potentially hundreds of drop records (mostly duplicate_url). Mitigation: this is acceptable for a reviewer artifact (it IS the funnel) and bounded by fetch_cap*queries; keep returned_urls per query but do NOT store full content (only URLs), so file size stays small. Optionally a PG_RETRIEVAL_TRACE_MAX env cap (LAW VI: configurable, not hardcoded) if size becomes a concern.

Emit-point drift: the funnel logic (dedup/scope/offtopic/cap/starved) is spread across run_live_retrieval; missing one emit point yields an incomplete trace (silent under-capture). Mitigation: the per-query return_count + per-kept + per-drop counts should reconcile against the existing LiveRetrievalResult counters (total_candidates_pre_filter, candidates_kept_by_offtopic, candidates_fetched/failed); a test asserts sum(kept)+sum(drop) ties out to total candidates so an omitted emit point fails loud.

Scope creep into the Path-B identity gate: must NOT route the new trace through assert_post_run or change record_retrieval_attempt semantics (the gate's required-backend-attempted check depends on it). Mitigation: keep the new sink entirely separate from _RETRIEVAL; the gate path is in the 'ALSO checked clean' list.

Bundling/conformance: if a reviewer expects retrieval_trace.jsonl inside the signed audit bundle, it needs a ContentType literal + conformance JSONL check (bundle_schema.py:41-48, conformance.py:65-77). Mitigation: ship the run-dir artifact first (no bundle change); add bundle inclusion as a small follow-up Issue if required, to keep this PR under the 200-LOC cap.

designs.area: Report quality â€” citation hygiene (#946 bare [ev_NNN] leak), verifier precision/recall (#948 trial_name over-strictness), and verified-only executive summary; Pipeline A (scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py + src/polaris_graph/generator/).

best_practices.practice: Match a cited entity (e.g. a clinical trial name) against the evidence DOCUMENT BODY/snippet via find-in-text, not just title/abstract; LLM-as-judge against title-only had 16-17% recall purely from terminology sensitivity, while body-level find_in_text recovers correct citations.
best_practices.source: CiteGuard: Faithful Citation Attribution for LLMs via Retrieval-Augmented Validation (ACL 2026), https://arxiv.org/html/2510.17853
best_practices.why: Directly the #948 root cause: trial_name_mismatch fires because _trial_names_in_evidence scans only statement+title. CiteGuard shows title-only matching is the precise cause of low recall and that body matching is the fix.

best_practices.practice: Distinguish a paper that merely CITES an entity from one that SUBSTANTIATES claims about it â€” body matching must be locality-aware, not 'appears anywhere in the document'.
best_practices.why: POLARIS's pass-7 fix excluded direct_quote because the SURMOUNT-3 paper references SURMOUNT-1; the safe body-matching design requires the trial name to co-occur in a LOCAL window with the sentence's content/numbers, preserving that precision while restoring recall.

best_practices.practice: Long-form factuality must be scored on BOTH precision (each claim entailed) AND recall (relevant facts covered); over-optimizing precision starves coverage (the ~48%-kept symptom).
best_practices.source: Beyond Precision: Importance-Aware Recall for Factuality Evaluation in Long-Form LLM Generation, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.03141; LongRecall, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.15085
best_practices.why: Frames #948: dropping well-framed correct prose on a title-only technicality is a recall failure that hurts the audit core without improving precision; the fix raises recall while holding precision via the locality constraint.

best_practices.practice: Citation hygiene: every in-text citation marker must resolve to a bibliography entry; unresolved/raw markers are a faithfulness defect, and verification/rendering must run an exact-form scrub-or-resolve pass over the published text.
best_practices.source: CiteGuard citation-attribution-alignment framing, https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.17853; RAG faithfulness eval (Cobbai), https://cobbai.com/blog/evaluate-rag-answers
best_practices.why: #946: a bare [ev_017] (no :start-end span) survives resolve_provenance_to_citations because the strip regex requires the span; the published report leaked an internal ID. Best practice = canonicalize/resolve every marker form in the final text.

best_practices.practice: Executive summary = 3-5 bullet key findings drawn ONLY from the verified body, stated up front, no new claims introduced; it is a digest of the report, not additional analysis.
best_practices.source: USC Research Guides executive-summary structure, https://libguides.usc.edu/writingguide/executivesummary; International AI Safety Report 2026 Executive Summary pattern, https://internationalaisafetyreport.org/publication/2026-report-executive-summary
best_practices.why: The exec-summary must inherit the audit guarantee. Composing it extractively from already-verified sentences means zero new verification burden and zero leak risk, while fixing the 'opens cold' complaint.

best_practices.practice: Reference-free citation/faithfulness checks should fail closed when no supporting window is found rather than fall back to whole-document matching (whole-doc entailment can be satisfied by an unrelated paragraph).
best_practices.source: Fast and Faithful: Real-Time Verification for Long-Document RAG, https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23508
best_practices.why: POLARIS already learned this (local-window vs whole-doc fallback in provenance_generator); the #948 body-match fix must follow the same fail-closed locality discipline, not loosen to 'name anywhere in doc'.

designs.proposed_solution: Three precise, fail-closed changes, all NO-SPEND / NO-NETWORK (pure string/regex + reuse of the existing local-window finder and already-loaded entailment judge): (a) #946 citation leak â€” in resolve_provenance_to_citations, after stripping spanned [#ev:...] tokens, also RESOLVE-or-SCRUB bare [ev_NNN] (and [ev_NNN:start-end]) markers: if the bare ev_id is in evidence_pool, route it through the same _num_for() so it becomes a real [N] citation; if not in pool, scrub it (drop the marker) and log+count it as a leak-prevented telemetry event. Apply the identical resolve-or-scrub to analyst_synthesis._scrub_ev_tokens (extend its regex to bare form; synthesis has no poolâ†’always scrub). (b) #948 trial-name recall â€” replace the title/statement-only _trial_names_in_evidence gate with a two-tier, locality-aware check: TIER 1 keep the existing authoritative title/statement match (fast path, full precision); TIER 2, ONLY when tier-1 misses, accept a trial name found in the evidence BODY (direct_quote) IFF that trial name occurs in a local â‰¤400-char window of direct_quote that ALSO contains â‰¥2 of the sentence's content words AND all of the sentence's required numeric atoms (reusing _find_local_support_window's locality discipline). This restores recall for correct body-supported claims while keeping the SURMOUNT-3â†’SURMOUNT-1 precision guard (a bare cross-reference far from the claim's numbers/words still fails). Emit per-decision telemetry (tier1_match / tier2_body_window_match / mismatch). (c) Exec summary â€” add an extractive, verified-only Executive Summary built from sr.verified_text sentences: select the 3-5 highest-signal verified sentences (those carrying numeric atoms and/or [N] citations, ranked by citation density, deduped), render them VERBATIM (markers intact) under a '## Key Findings' heading inserted between the report title and the first section. Because every bullet is a verbatim already-verified+resolved sentence, it introduces zero new claims and carries the audit guarantee by construction; omit the block (no empty heading) when fewer than 2 qualifying sentences exist.

designs.area: Semantic memory (#949): wire verified-claim reuse (query_related_claims) back into Pipeline A generation, lexical-first, anti-poisoning + fail-closed preserved

best_practices.practice: Treat agent-confirmed/verified facts as first-class write-path memory and reuse them across sessions (single-pass ADD; agent confirmations weighted equally to source statements).
best_practices.why: mem0 2026 reports +29.6 pts temporal and +23.1 pts multi-hop reasoning when valid prior facts are reused. POLARIS already WRITES verified claims (verified_claim_graph.py) but never reads them back â€” capturing the documented write-path win means wiring query_related_claims into generation.

best_practices.practice: Use multi-signal retrieval (keyword + semantic + entity) but START with lexical/keyword recall; embeddings are an add-on, not a prerequisite.
best_practices.why: No single signal dominates; lexical+semantic fusion cut tokens ~75% (LoCoMo 92.5 at ~6.9k tok/query). Justifies shipping the existing lexical query_related_claims first (no-spend, no-network) and deferring ChromaDB embeddings to a stretch Issue.

best_practices.practice: Flat experience records without relational structure can introduce noisy retrieval, unreliable reuse, and can HURT accuracy vs no-memory; clinical reasoning needs graph/relational + quality gating.
best_practices.why: GSEM (clinical self-evolving memory) and arXiv 2604.22085 warn flat keyword stores degrade. POLARIS's KG is flat SQLite + lexical keyword. Mitigation: make reuse OPTIONAL, fail-closed, default-off, advisory-only (never earns provenance) so a noisy recall cannot lower quality below today's baseline.

best_practices.practice: Defend memory against poisoning: verified-only ingestion, provenance tags, contradiction detection, immutable audit logs; scope retrieval to verified agent-internal outputs only.
best_practices.why: OWASP Agentic Top-10 ASI08 + MINJA (>95% injection success). POLARIS's reusable==1 (VERIFIED-only) filter, role_verdicts provenance, audit-only rows, and find_contradictions already implement four of these. The design must PRESERVE them when adding the read path â€” a non-VERIFIED row must never become reusable.

best_practices.practice: Track temporal validity / supersession; discard or down-weight stale facts; carry provenance so the agent calibrates confidence and avoids overconfident assertions on old data.
best_practices.why: Staleness in high-relevance facts is the unsolved, lethal-in-clinical failure (a dose true last question can be wrong this question). Resolves to: reused claims are ADVISORY priming only and must be RE-GROUNDED in this question's evidence by strict_verify â€” a stale reused claim that cannot be re-grounded is dropped, not asserted.

best_practices.practice: Temporal knowledge graph with validity windows / non-destructive supersession (Zep) beats vector-only for evolving facts (15-pt LongMemEval gap); but vectors+entity-boost suffice without graph infra for most deployments.
best_practices.why: Justifies the STRETCH ladder: lexical (ship) -> embeddings -> edges/temporal-validity. POLARIS need not build a Neo4j-class graph now; the cheapest correct path is lexical reuse + the existing contradiction hook, with edges/temporal as a later Issue.

current_state.what: WRITE-ONLY in practice. Flat SQLite (verified_claims table, no edges). write_claim persists ALL verdicts but reusable=1 only for VERIFIED (anti-poisoning). query_related_claims (lexical keyword overlap + substring, VERIFIED-only) and find_contradictions (negation-polarity + numeric-divergence stub) EXIST and are tested but are read by nothing in the pipeline.

The writer. Lines 254-316: opens VerifiedClaimGraphStore(run_dir=run_dir) and write_claim per claim during run_four_role_evaluation (the LAST pipeline step). Never calls query_related_claims/find_contradictions. KG is opened under PER-QUESTION run_dir, so the snowball is born and dies inside one question.

5 questions run in ONE process: for q in queries_to_run (line 3596) under a single out_root (3552-3554). run_dir = out_root/domain/slug (1322) is per-question. An operator_review_queue.jsonl append pattern already exists (1130) and is reusable for contradiction surfacing. SWEEP_QUERIES default set is topically diverse (tirzepatide, afib, FDA-AI, Medicare, RAG...) with a few overlaps (two tirzepatide slugs; Novo/obesity).

Pure builder: turns verified sentences (kept_sentences_pre_resolve) into FourRoleClaim inputs after strict_verify. Confirms claims are built from sentences that ALREADY passed strict_verify against THIS question's evidence pool â€” the natural fail-closed boundary that reuse priming must respect.

ChromaDB LTM-Global embedding store (promote_to_ltm/query_ltm). Best-effort, Pipeline-B only, not wired into Pipeline A. Its default embedder downloads/runs a model -> NOT no-network. Candidate for the embeddings STRETCH only, kept out of the no-spend shipping fix.

generate_multi_section_report (3606): outline stage (_call_outline) then per-section LLM calls; strict_verify runs inside, producing kept_sentences. The outline/planning altitude is the safest injection seam for advisory prior knowledge (primes structure without entering citable per-section prose directly).

designs.proposed_solution: Wire query_related_claims into Pipeline A generation as OPTIONAL, fail-closed, advisory prior-knowledge priming that earns NO provenance â€” plus a paired read-only contradiction safety seam. Three buildable parts, all no-spend / no-network. (1) CAMPAIGN-SCOPE the KG: add an explicit campaign_db_path (out_root-scoped, e.g. out_root/verified_claim_graph.sqlite) so the snowball persists across the 5 questions in the one sweep process; pass it into run_four_role_evaluation (default stays per-run when unset, so existing tests are byte-stable). (2) SEAM G (the #949 ask): before per-question generation, open the campaign KG read-only and call query_related_claims(research_question); fence the returned VERIFIED claims into the OUTLINE/planning prompt under a hard banner: 'Verified in OTHER reports â€” NOT evidence for THIS report; do not cite; every claim here MUST be re-grounded in the numbered evidence below.' This primes recall/structure but earns no provenance â€” strict_verify is untouched, so any primed claim not re-groundable in THIS question's evidence pool is DROPPED exactly as today. Gate behind PG_KG_REUSE_ENABLED (default 0). (3) SEAM C (paired safety): call find_contradictions(research_question) against the campaign reuse pool and append flags to operator_review_queue.jsonl (reusing the existing 1130 pattern) for the Â§-1.1 human/Codex audit; read-only, never feeds generation, guards exactly the contamination Seam G could introduce.
